info.title: Wanderer's Guide API
info.description: Public HTTP API for Wanderer's Guide, the Pathfinder 2e character builder. Every endpoint is a Supabase Edge Function reached via `POST /functions/v1/<name>`. All responses follow the [JSend](https://github.com/omniti-labs/jsend) convention: `{ status: 'success' | 'fail' | 'error', data?, message? }`.

supabaseUrl.description: Base URL of your Supabase instance, including scheme and port.

tags.name: Content
tags.description: Pathfinder 2e content: spells, items, feats, ancestries, classes, etc.

tags.name: GM
tags.description: Game-master tools: group management, access codes.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Public user profiles, account management, Patreon integration.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Full-text and advanced search across content.

apiKeyAuth.description: API key created in your Wanderer's Guide account settings. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Used for direct API access from external tools and scripts.

bearerAuth.description: Supabase Auth JWT issued to the signed-in user. Used by the Wanderer's Guide web app. Subject to the same row-level security policies as the user's database access.

Operation.description: An entry in the operations engine. See the Content Model guide. Shape varies by `type`; treated as opaque JSON by the API.

ContentFilter.description: Common filter shape for find-* endpoints.

id.description: Single id or array of ids. When omitted (or array), the response is an array. When a single id, the response is the matching object or null.

content_sources.description: Restrict to these content_source_ids. When omitted, only official published sources are returned.

traits.description: Match rows whose `traits` array contains all of these trait ids.

JSendFail.description: Operation failed due to invalid input or insufficient permissions.

post.description: Look up spells by id, name, content source, or trait. Omit `id` to get an array; pass a single integer `id` to get one row or `null`.
Look up items by id, name, or content source.

post.summary: Find ability blocks (feats, actions, class features, etc.)
post.description: Ability blocks cover feats, actions, physical-features, senses, class-features, heritages, and modes. Filter by `type` to narrow.
Returns published official sources by default. Pass `homebrew: true` to include user-created sources.
Pass `id` to update an existing row, omit `id` (or set to `-1`) to insert a new one.

200.description: JSend success. Inserted row on create, `true` on update.

post.summary: Create or update an ability block (feat, action, etc.)

post.description: Auto-creates the associated ancestry trait if `trait_id` is missing.

post.summary: Create or update a class archetype
Create or update a content source (book)
post.description: Creating a new homebrew content source requires Patreon tier 2+. Updating an existing source requires ownership.

post.summary: Submit a content update / errata for community review
post.description: Creates a content_update record in PENDING state. Discord moderators approve/reject via the bot, which calls `/update-content-update`.

ref_id.description: Required for UPDATE/DELETE actions; the id of the existing row.

200.description: JSend success with the created update row.

post.summary: Create or update a versatile heritage

post.description: Deletes any single row from a content table. Caller must be the row owner (or owner of the row's content source) or an admin/mod. Anyone else gets a JSend `fail`.

200.description: JSend envelope. `success` if deleted, `fail` if forbidden, `error` for invalid type or unknown row.

post.description: When called with an API key, the caller must have a `client_access` grant on the requested character (set via the user UI). Without a grant, returns 403. Filtering by `campaign_id` only returns all characters in that campaign so members can see each other.
Free users are limited to 6 characters. Patreon tier 2+ removes the cap. Pass `id` to update.
Same body shape as `create-character` but `id` is required. Forbidden keys (`id`, `created_at`, `content_source_id`, `user_id`) are stripped server-side.
Creating a new campaign requires Patreon tier 1+ (free users cannot create campaigns).
Creating new encounters requires Patreon tier 1+.

post.summary: Remove a character (and its player) from a campaign

post.description: Only the campaign owner (GM) can reset the key. Existing join codes immediately stop working.

200.description: JSend success with the new key.

post.summary: Join a Game Master's virtual-tier group
post.description: A GM with the GAME-MASTER Patreon tier shares an `access_code` so members of their group inherit Patreon perks. The caller exchanges the code for membership.

post.summary: Remove a user from your GM group
post.description: Caller must be the GM. Removes the named user from the caller's virtual-tier group.

post.summary: List the users in your GM group

post.description: Invalidates the previous code; existing members stay in the group.

Without a body, returns the caller's own profile. Pass `id` (Supabase auth user id, UUID) or `_id` (numeric `public_user.id`) to look up another user. Sensitive fields (Patreon tokens, API keys) are stripped from the response.

200.description: JSend success with the public user.

post.description: Updates a subset of `public_user` fields for the authenticated caller. Accepts `display_name`, `summary`, `image_url`, `background_image_url`, `organized_play_id`, `site_theme`, `subscribed_content_sources`, `api`. The user id and timestamps are never writable.
Permanently deletes the authenticated user, their public_user row, and the auth identity. **Irreversible.**
Receives the OAuth `code` from Patreon's redirect, exchanges it for tokens, and stores them on the caller's `public_user.patreon` field.

post.summary: Get usage stats for a content source
post.description: Returns the source itself plus the count of subscribers (users with this source enabled).

Two modes:
- **Simple**: set `text` (>= 2 chars). Runs a websearch full-text query against every content table, capped at 20 hits each. Best for spotlight-style search.
- **Advanced**: set `is_advanced: true` plus a `type` and any of the per-type filters. Returns matching rows in the corresponding bucket only.

All responses include all twelve content buckets, even if empty.

200.description: JSend success with the SearchResults envelope.

post.summary: Upload a portrait or background image
post.description: Accepts a base64-encoded image. Patreon tier 1+ required. Files larger than ~5 MB are rejected.

200.description: JSend envelope. On success, `data` contains the uploaded file's public URL.

post.summary: Query the vector database for semantic search
post.description: Proxies to the WG vector DB (Chroma). Use for semantic / nearest-neighbor lookups across spell or feat names and descriptions.

post.summary: Populate vector embeddings for a collection
post.description: Indexer endpoint: takes a collection name (`name` or `content`), a content type, and a list of row ids; generates embeddings and stores them. Used by content-update flows; not generally called by external clients.

post.summary: (Discord webhook) Approve, reject, or vote on a content update
post.description: Service-to-service webhook for the WG Discord moderation bot. Authenticates via the shared `CONTENT_UPDATE_KEY` secret in the `Authorization: Bearer <secret>` header (NOT a user JWT or API key). When state=APPROVE, applies the underlying content change.
